trivia_bullets.text: A Room card — the major mechanical innovation of Duskmourn: House of Horror (DSK, released September 27, 2024). Rooms are split permanent enchantments; each half is a door unlocked separately. The mechanic began development as a Mansion deck with a Meeple token but was streamlined into the current permanent-split form.
sources: magic.wizards.com: Top of the Duskmourning Part 1

trivia_bullets.text: The two doors complement by role: Derelict Attic (2B) draws two cards at cost of 2 life; Widow's Walk (3B) grants +1/+0 and deathtouch to a solo attacker. The cheaper half powers development, the expensive half closes games — the canonical DSK Room design relationship per Rosewater. Art by Marc Simonetti, French concept artist (born 1977) with film credits including Valerian and the City of a Thousand Planets, Aladdin, Maleficent 2, and Aquaman 2; 23+ MTG cards.
sources: magic.wizards.com: Top of the Duskmourning Part 1

trivia_bullets.text: EDHREC rank 7,999 — highest Commander adoption among DSK cards in this batch, appearing in approximately 12,800 of 2.91 million Commander decks (0.44% inclusion). Draw-2-lose-2 provides utility in black Commander decks; deathtouch-on-attacker plays in voltron and mono-black aggressive strategies.

"Widow's Walk" is a real architectural term for a railed rooftop platform historically built on coastal New England homes, legendarily for wives to watch for returning ships. The card inverts this: the Widow's Walk here is a vantage point where attackers become lethal, mapping the interior/exterior duality of the House onto a single split card.

related_cards.name: Keys to the House
related_cards.note: Unlocks locked Room doors without mana cost; direct mechanical interaction

related_cards.name: Marina Vendrell
related_cards.note: One of two DSK cards that can lock an unlocked door, enabling re-trigger of unlock effects

related_cards.name: Ominous Haunt // Locus of Enlightenment
related_cards.note: Another black Room card for comparison in Limited evaluation
